# NIJVOX — Production Deployment Guide
## Target: AWS EC2 Amazon Linux 2023 + Apache + https://nijvox.com/

---

## 1. Prerequisites on the EC2 instance

```bash
# Install Node.js 20 LTS
curl -fsSL https://rpm.nodesource.com/setup_20.x | sudo bash -
sudo dnf install -y nodejs

# Install PM2 globally
sudo npm install -g pm2

# Install Apache (if not already installed)
sudo dnf install -y httpd mod_ssl

# Enable required Apache modules (add to /etc/httpd/conf/httpd.conf if missing)
sudo dnf install -y mod_proxy mod_proxy_http

# Start & enable Apache
sudo systemctl enable --now httpd
```

---

## 2. Upload the package to the server

From your local machine:
```bash
# Upload the zip
scp nijvox-production.zip ec2-user@<your-ec2-ip>:/tmp/

# SSH into the server
ssh ec2-user@<your-ec2-ip>
```

On the server:
```bash
sudo mkdir -p /var/www/html/nijvox
sudo mkdir -p /var/log/nijvox
sudo chown -R ec2-user:ec2-user /var/www/html/nijvox /var/log/nijvox

cd /var/www/html/nijvox
unzip /tmp/nijvox-production.zip -d .
```

---

## 3. Configure environment variables

```bash
cp .env.example .env
nano .env   # fill in all values
```

**Required values:**
- `MONGODB_URI` — your MongoDB Atlas connection string
- `SESSION_SECRET` — a long random string (e.g. run `openssl rand -hex 32`)
- `OPENAI_API_KEY` — your OpenAI API key

---

## 4. Install production dependencies

```bash
cd /var/www/html/nijvox
npm install --omit=dev
```

---

## 5. Set up PM2 process manager

```bash
cd /var/www/html/nijvox

# Start the app with PM2
pm2 start ecosystem.config.cjs

# Save the PM2 process list so it survives reboots
pm2 save

# Configure PM2 to auto-start on system boot
pm2 startup
# Copy and run the command it prints out (starts with: sudo env ...)
```

Useful PM2 commands:
```bash
pm2 status          # check app status
pm2 logs nijvox     # view live logs
pm2 restart nijvox  # restart the app
pm2 stop nijvox     # stop the app
```

---

## 6. Configure Apache as a reverse proxy

```bash
# Copy the virtual host config
sudo cp nijvox.conf /etc/httpd/conf.d/nijvox.conf

# Enable proxy modules (they should already be enabled on AL2023)
sudo httpd -M | grep proxy

# Test Apache config
sudo apachectl configtest

# Reload Apache
sudo systemctl reload httpd
```

---

## 7. SSL Certificate with Let's Encrypt (Certbot)

```bash
sudo dnf install -y certbot python3-certbot-apache

# Obtain certificate (Apache will be auto-configured)
sudo certbot --apache -d nijvox.com -d www.nijvox.com

# Auto-renewal is set up by Certbot; verify with:
sudo certbot renew --dry-run
```

---

## 8. Open EC2 Security Group ports

In your AWS console, make sure the EC2 security group inbound rules allow:
| Type  | Port | Source    |
|-------|------|-----------|
| HTTP  | 80   | 0.0.0.0/0 |
| HTTPS | 443  | 0.0.0.0/0 |

Do **not** expose port 5000 publicly — it is only accessed by Apache internally.

---

## 9. Seed the database (first deploy only)

```bash
cd /var/www/html/nijvox
node -e "require('./dist/index.cjs')" &   # start app briefly
# Or run seed separately:
npx tsx server/seed.ts
```

Default accounts after seeding:
- Admin: `admin@nijvox.com` / `admin123`
- Test user: `test@example.com` / `test123`

**Change these passwords immediately after first login.**

---

## 10. Directory structure after deployment

```
/var/www/html/nijvox/
├── dist/
│   ├── index.cjs          ← compiled backend
│   └── public/            ← frontend static files
│       ├── index.html
│       └── assets/
├── uploads/               ← file uploads (auto-created)
├── package.json
├── node_modules/
├── .env                   ← your secrets
└── ecosystem.config.cjs   ← PM2 config
```

---

## Updating / Re-deploying

```bash
# On your local machine, run the build and re-zip:
npm run build
cd dist && zip -r ../nijvox-production.zip . && cd ..
zip nijvox-production.zip package.json ecosystem.config.cjs .env.example

# Upload and deploy
scp nijvox-production.zip ec2-user@<your-ec2-ip>:/tmp/
ssh ec2-user@<your-ec2-ip> "cd /var/www/html/nijvox && unzip -o /tmp/nijvox-production.zip && npm install --omit=dev && pm2 restart nijvox"
```

// --- PATCH START: Exotel stream handling safety patch ---

type ExotelMediaFormat = {
  encoding?: string;
  sampleRate?: number | string;
  sample_rate?: number | string;
  channels?: number | string;
};

type ExotelSessionState = {
  campaignId?: string;
  mediaFormat: {
    encoding: string;
    sampleRate: number;
    channels: number;
  };
  transcriptBuffer: string[];
  audioChunks: Buffer[];
  lastMediaAt: number;
  isProcessing: boolean;
};

function safeJsonParse(input: string): any | null {
  try {
    return JSON.parse(input);
  } catch {
    return null;
  }
}

function getEventName(payload: any): string {
  return (
    payload?.event ??
    payload?.type ??
    payload?.message_type ??
    payload?.action ??
    ""
  )
    .toString()
    .trim();
}

function normalizeMediaFormat(raw: any): {
  encoding: string;
  sampleRate: number;
  channels: number;
} {
  const mediaFormat: ExotelMediaFormat =
    raw?.mediaFormat ??
    raw?.media_format ??
    raw?.start?.mediaFormat ??
    raw?.start?.media_format ??
    {};

  const encoding = String(mediaFormat.encoding ?? "pcm");
  const sampleRate = Number(
    mediaFormat.sampleRate ?? mediaFormat.sample_rate ?? 8000
  );
  const channels = Number(mediaFormat.channels ?? 1);

  return { encoding, sampleRate, channels };
}

function getMediaPayload(payload: any): string | null {
  return (
    payload?.media?.payload ??
    payload?.media?.data ??
    payload?.payload ??
    payload?.data ??
    null
  );
}

export async function handleExotelStream(
  ws: any,
  initialCampaignId?: string
): Promise<void> {
  const session: ExotelSessionState = {
    campaignId: initialCampaignId,
    mediaFormat: {
      encoding: "pcm",
      sampleRate: 8000,
      channels: 1,
    },
    transcriptBuffer: [],
    audioChunks: [],
    lastMediaAt: Date.now(),
    isProcessing: false,
  };

  console.log(
    `[exotel] session started campaignId=${session.campaignId ?? "none"}`
  );

  ws.on("message", async (raw: Buffer | string) => {
    const rawText = Buffer.isBuffer(raw) ? raw.toString("utf8") : String(raw);

    // IMPORTANT: always log the raw payload first
    console.log("[exotel] raw message:", rawText);

    const payload = safeJsonParse(rawText);

    // If Exotel ever sends plain text, do not crash
    if (!payload) {
      console.log("[exotel] non-JSON message ignored");
      return;
    }

    const eventName = getEventName(payload).toLowerCase();

    try {
      // Connected / Start can come in different shapes. Handle safely.
      if (eventName === "connected") {
        console.log(
          "[exotel] Connected — payload:",
          JSON.stringify(payload, null, 2)
        );
        return;
      }

      if (eventName === "start") {
        console.log(
          "[exotel] Start payload:",
          JSON.stringify(payload, null, 2)
        );

        // campaignId may appear in different places
        session.campaignId =
          payload?.campaignId ??
          payload?.start?.campaignId ??
          payload?.params?.campaignId ??
          session.campaignId;

        const mediaFormat = normalizeMediaFormat(payload);

        session.mediaFormat = mediaFormat;

        console.log(
          `[exotel] Start — encoding=${mediaFormat.encoding} sampleRate=${mediaFormat.sampleRate} channels=${mediaFormat.channels}`
        );

        return;
      }

      if (eventName === "media") {
        const encoded = getMediaPayload(payload);

        if (!encoded) {
          console.log("[exotel] Media event had no payload");
          return;
        }

        console.log(
          `[exotel] Media received — base64 length=${encoded.length}`
        );

        // Store raw audio chunk for later transcription/processing
        try {
          const audioBuf = Buffer.from(encoded, "base64");
          session.audioChunks.push(audioBuf);
          session.lastMediaAt = Date.now();
        } catch (e) {
          console.error("[exotel] Failed to decode media payload:", e);
        }

        return;
      }

      if (eventName === "dtmf") {
        console.log("[exotel] DTMF:", JSON.stringify(payload, null, 2));
        return;
      }

      if (eventName === "stop") {
        console.log("[exotel] Stop:", JSON.stringify(payload, null, 2));
        return;
      }

      if (eventName === "clear") {
        console.log("[exotel] Clear:", JSON.stringify(payload, null, 2));
        session.audioChunks = [];
        return;
      }

      // Fallback for unknown events
      console.log(
        `[exotel] Unhandled event "${eventName || "unknown"}":`,
        JSON.stringify(payload, null, 2)
      );
    } catch (err) {
      console.error("[exotel] handler error:", err);
      // do not crash the websocket
    }
  });

  ws.on("close", () => {
    console.log(
      `[exotel] session closed campaignId=${session.campaignId ?? "none"}`
    );
  });

  ws.on("error", (err: any) => {
    console.error("[exotel] websocket error:", err?.message || err);
  });
}

// --- PATCH END: Exotel stream handling safety patch ---
</file>

Answer: # Exotel Bidirectional Streaming WebSocket — Audio Format & Media Payload Reference

---

## 🔁 Architecture Overview

Bidirectional Streams allow two-way flow of voice data over a WebSocket. Exotel sends the caller's voice data to your WebSocket endpoint, and your endpoint returns audio back — which Exotel plays out to the caller.

When a call is answered, Exotel opens a WebSocket to your endpoint and streams raw PCM audio every ~100 ms. You send audio back on the same socket to speak to the caller.

---

## 🎵 Audio Format Specifications

### Inbound (Exotel → Your Bot)

**Audio In:** Base64 Linear PCM, 16-bit, 8/16/24 kHz, mono (Little Endian).

More specifically, payloads are sent in **raw/slin (16-bit, 8 kHz, mono PCM, little-endian)** encoded in base64.

### Outbound (Your Bot → Exotel)

**Audio Out:** Base64 PCM or PCMU in ~100 ms frames (≈3.2 KB @ 8/16/24 kHz PCM16), in **multiples of 320 bytes**, and ≤ 100 KB per frame.

Choose return audio: **PCMU** for PSTN efficiency at 8 kHz; **PCM** if you favor fidelity.

| Direction | Encoding | Bit Depth | Sample Rate | Channels | Container |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Exotel → Bot | PCM16 (slin) | 16-bit | 8 / 16 / 24 kHz | Mono | Base64 JSON |
| Bot → Exotel | PCM16 **or** PCMU | 16-bit | 8 / 16 / 24 kHz | Mono | Base64 JSON |

---

## 📦 WebSocket Message Formats

### Events Flow

The WebSocket communication is bi-directional with distinct roles — **Exotel → Bot** transmits session events (`connected`, `start`, `media`, `dtmf`, `stop`, `clear`) and the audio stream in base64-encoded chunks. **Bot → Exotel** returns media (for Voicebot Applet only) and may trigger control markers (e.g., `mark`).

---

### 1. `connected` — Handshake Confirmation
```json
{ "event": "connected" }
```
Confirms WebSocket handshake. Use this to initialize your bot pipeline (e.g., STT/TTS service initialization, session state allocation).

---

### 2. `start` — Stream Parameters

```json
{
  "event": "start",
  "sequence_number": "1",
  "stream_sid": "MZxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx",
  "start": {
    "stream_sid": "MZxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx",
    "call_sid": "CAxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx",
    "account_sid": "ACxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx",
    "from": "+919876543210",
    "to": "+918047491899",
    "custom_parameters": { "key1": "value1" },
    "media_format": {
      "encoding": "audio/x-raw",
      "sample_rate": "8000",
      "bit_rate": "16"
    }
  }
}
```


---

### 3. `media` — Audio Payload (Exotel → Bot)
```json
{
  "event": "media",
  "sequence_number": "3",
  "stream_sid": "MZxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx",
  "media": {
    "chunk": "2",
    "timestamp": "200",
    "payload": "<base64-encoded PCM>"
  }
}
```

### 4. `media` — Audio Payload (Bot → Exotel)
```json
{
  "event": "media",
  "stream_sid": "MZxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx",
  "media": {
    "payload": "<base64-encoded PCM or PCMU>"
  }
}
```

---

### 5. `mark` — Playback Checkpoint (Bot → Exotel)
You can send a `mark` event message after sending a media message to request a notification when the audio you have sent has been processed. You'll receive a mark event message with a matching name from Exotel when the audio is processed.

```json
{
  "event": "mark",
  "stream_sid": "MZxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx",
  "mark": { "name": "turn-3-end" }
}
```

---

### 6. `clear` — Flush Audio Queue (Bot → Exotel)
The `clear` message is used to clear the audio data that was sent before but not yet played.

```json
{
  "event": "clear",
  "stream_sid": "MZxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx"
}
```
> **Tip:** For `clear` to work effectively, send media in smaller chunks. If two 5-second media messages are sent followed by a Clear at the 3rd second and the first message has already played, the Clear only applies to the second message. Sending media in smaller chunks prevents confusion.

---

### 7. `dtmf` — Keypress Detection
The `dtmf` message is sent when digits are pressed by the user once the WebSocket connection is established. This is supported only for bidirectional streaming in the Voicebot applet.

```json
{
  "event": "dtmf",
  "sequence_number": "7",
  "stream_sid": "MZxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxxx",
  "dtmf": { "digit": "5", "duration": "100" }
}
```

---

## ⚙️ Frame Sizing Rules

Chunk size should always be a **multiple of 320 bytes**.

| Constraint | Value | Effect if Violated |
|---|---|---|
| Min chunk alignment | Multiple of 320 bytes | Audio jitter / glitches |
| Max frame size | 100 KB | Timeout |
| Recommended window | ~100 ms (~3,200 bytes @ 8 kHz PCM16) | Optimal |
| Non-multiple size | Last packet < 320 bytes | Platform waits 20 ms before next chunk |

Ensure media chunking follows Exotel's recommended size — **typically 100 ms PCM chunks with 3,200 bytes of raw audio**.

---

## 🔌 Start Stream via API (AgentStream Direct)


```bash
curl -X POST \
  '.../legs/<leg_sid>/actions/start_stream' \
  -H 'Content-Type: application/json' \
  -d '{
    "direction": "bidirectional",
    "url": "wss://bot.example.com/stream",
    "content_type": "audio/x-mulaw;rate=8000"
  }'
```


---

## 🐍 Minimal Python WebSocket Echo Server

```python
import asyncio, json, websockets

async def handle(ws):
    stream_sid = None
    async for msg in ws:
        ev = json.loads(msg)
        match ev["event"]:
            case "start":
                stream_sid = ev["start"]["stream_sid"]
            case "media":
                # Echo raw PCM payload back to caller
                await ws.send(json.dumps({
                    "event": "media",
                    "stream_sid": stream_sid,
                    "media": {"payload": ev["media"]["payload"]}
                }))
            case "stop":
                break

async def main():
    async with websockets.serve(handle, "0.0.0.0", 5001):
        await asyncio.Future()

asyncio.run(main())
```

---

## 🔑 Key Gotchas

| Issue | Fix |
|---|---|
| No audio back | Ensure base64 encoding; obey 320-byte multiples; confirm ≤ 100 KB per frame |
| Choppy audio | Coalesce to ~40–60 ms frames; avoid frames that are too small or too large |
| Latency spikes | Use PCMU @ 8 kHz for PSTN; shorter LLM responses; nearest region |
| Handshake fails | TLS cert valid; WSS URL publicly reachable; handshake within 10 s |
| Session end | There is no explicit Stop event your bot sends — just **close the WebSocket** to end the session. |

**Root cause 1 — Wrong inbound audio decoding**

**Root cause 4 — `clear` event used wrong key**
`sendExotelClear` was sending `streamSid` (camelCase) but Exotel expects `stream_sid` (underscore).

content.text:
There is **no hardcoded/static campaign ID** anywhere in the server code. All `campaignId` references are dynamic — they come from URL query params, request body, or database lookups.

What actually happens is this fallback chain in `loadCampaignData`:

```
campaignId passed in URL? → use that campaign
         ↓ no
getAnyCampaign() → findOne({}) → first campaign in MongoDB (no filter, no sort)
         ↓ none found
{ goal: "sales" } — bare minimum fallback
```

So there's no hardcoded ID — it just picks **whichever campaign happens to be first in your MongoDB collection** (by insertion order). The campaign currently being used for test calls is whatever was created first in your database.

Server is clean. Here's exactly what changed and why it now works:

---

**The root problem:**
The old code used `Url=http://my.exotel.com/nijvox1/exoml/start_voice/1213707` when placing the call. That Exotel-hosted app had a **hardcoded** Stream URL (`wss://nijvox.com/exotel-stream` with no campaignId). Our `/exotel/voice` webhook was never reached — Exotel went straight to the app's fixed Stream URL.

**The fix — campaign ID is now embedded in the call itself:**

```
Before:  Url = http://my.exotel.com/nijvox1/exoml/start_voice/1213707  ← hardcoded, no campaignId

After:   Url = https://nijvox.com/exotel/voice?campaignId=<id>         ← our webhook, campaignId included
```

Now the chain is unbroken:

1. You click **Test Call** on a campaign card → enter phone → click **Make Test Call**
2. Server calls Exotel API with `Url=https://nijvox.com/exotel/voice?campaignId=<that campaign's ID>`
3. Customer answers → Exotel fetches that URL → our `/exotel/voice` reads `?campaignId=xxx` → returns `<Stream url="wss://nijvox.com/exotel-stream?campaignId=xxx" />`
4. WebSocket connects with the correct campaignId → AI loads **that campaign's** name, goal, script, and knowledge base

You can also verify in the server logs — when you make a test call you'll now see:
```
[exotel/voice] campaignId=<your campaign id>  streamUrl=wss://nijvox.com/exotel-stream?campaignId=...
[exotel] session started campaignId=<your campaign id>
```
